demographic_audit_note: Audit of Atlantis vs. facility face sheet revealed a zip code mismatch (19143 vs 19104). Corrected in Atlantis to ensure the HHA referral was routed to the correct catchment area. Verified Medicare Part B as primary.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker confirmed the goal is Home with HHA services for renal monitoring and PT. Patient has a stable home environment with a local caregiver (daughter).
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: Yes, saw the logo in the lobby. Q2: Thinks we are 'discharge helpers.' Q3: SW mentioned home care but patient was unclear on the MA role. Q4: Expressed high anxiety that the nurse won't show up on time, leaving her alone with her dialysis port — escalated this anxiety to the SW.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient completed final round of acute stabilization. SW confirms discharge target is Friday. PT notes patient is now 'supervision only' for transfers.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into daughter's iPhone; program flyer with PN contact information delivered to patient's bedside folder.
hha_confirmation_status: HHA (Reliant Home Health) confirmed 'In Place' status on Thursday. SOC date: Friday; Nurse: Sarah T.; First-visit window: 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM. Barrier identified: None at time of booking.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed Friday D/C is firm and Reliant HHA has the orders. Call 2 (to daughter): Confirmed daughter will be present for the 10:00 AM HHA window and knows to call the PN if the nurse is 30 minutes late.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached patient 24 hours prior to D/C. Patient confirmed home is ready and dialysis transport for Monday is booked. Re-confirmed HHA visit time.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered D/C date (Friday) and 1st visit date (Friday) into Atlantis. Physical appointment card handed to patient during final bedside visit.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for Friday at 2:00 PM, allowing a two-hour buffer after the HHA's confirmed 12:00 PM window closure. Booking occurred only after HHA was 'In Place'.
narrative_summary: Martha G. was a high-risk renal transition where the Connection Intake revealed significant anxiety regarding home care reliability. The PN successfully built a Confidence Loop by verifying HHA 'In Place' status and conducting a dual-call Confidence Audit. However, on the day of discharge, the HHA nurse failed to appear during the confirmed window (Day 1 Home No-Show). Despite the PN following all HHA-First scheduling rules, the transition failed when the MA arrived at a home where skilled intake had not yet occurred.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Atlantis demographic audit reveals incorrect zip code for HHA routing.
Connection Intake Q4 reveals patient fear of nurse no-shows.
HHA nurse fails to arrive for the scheduled Friday morning SOC visit.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient expresses fear of being abandoned by the HHA on Day 1.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Conducted Connection Intake Q4 and escalated anxiety to the SW for agency vetting.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA status is pending without an assigned nurse name.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Deferred MA scheduling and called HHA intake coordinator to identify the staffing blocker.

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter is unsure of who to contact if the HHA is late.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Performed Confidence Audit Call 2 and inserted PN V-Card into phone.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Upon learning of the HHA no-show from the daughter, the PN logs the delay in Atlantis and waits for the Social Worker to contact the agency for a status update the following Monday.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW's role as the primary agency liaison but risks a total transition breakdown over the weekend.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN calls the HHA intake coordinator to identify the specific cause of the no-show, discovers a nurse car breakdown, and immediately flags this to the SW to determine if the patient is safe or needs a Saturday bridge visit.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Functions as a liaison by identifying the specific barrier and providing the SW with actionable data to resolve the care gap.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN calls a secondary HHA agency that they personally know has weekend staff and asks them to 'pick up' the Martha G. referral immediately to save the Friday transition.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Directly violates the SW Boundary by suggesting and initiating a referral to an alternative agency.
